lesson1.humanSituation: A scooter dealer sells vehicles on instalments and needs cash to restock before the instalments arrive.
lesson1.openingPuzzle: A lender can have thousands of paying borrowers and still lack enough cash to make new loans today.
lesson1.prediction: Choose between waiting for the instalments, borrowing against the business, or selling a defined right to future payments.
lesson1.misconception: Securitisation makes loans safer or removes every risk from the originator.
lesson1.definition: A financing transaction in which identified financial assets or their cash flows are pooled and used to support securities issued to investors.
lesson1.namedEvidence: Kogta Financial and the vehicle-loan receivables transferred into the transaction reported by ICRA, including the ninety-crore-rupee pool principal, one-hundred-and-fifteen-crore-rupee receivables, and eighty-one-crore-rupee senior notes.
lesson1.evidenceLimit: Public rating material supports the reported structure and later collateral support but does not replace the full pool tape, transfer documents, servicing agreement, account controls, or executable waterfall.
lesson1.transferSituation: A solar-panel installer sells systems on instalments and considers financing the contracted customer payments.
lesson1.closingPrinciple: Securitisation does not make weak loans strong. It changes who funds them, who receives their cash, and who suffers when that cash fails.

episode1.humanSituation: A family makes one mortgage payment that ultimately supports an investor claim.
episode1.openingPuzzle: The payment is real, yet each handoff can weaken the right, information, control, or timing on which the investor relies.
episode1.prediction: Distinguish ordinary lender borrowing from financing a defined pool of mortgage cash flows.
episode1.controllingObject: One mortgage payment moving through the financing architecture.
episode1.misconception: Pooling, transfer, a rating, or a senior label automatically improves borrower quality.
episode1.definition: Securitisation converts identified asset cash flows into investable claims through transfer, issuance, servicing, control, and payment priority.
episode1.namedEvidence: The U.S. mortgage market before the 2008 crisis and the originate-to-distribute incentive problem described by the Financial Crisis Inquiry Commission.
episode1.evidenceLimit: The historical case demonstrates mechanisms and incentive failures but cannot determine the quality of another transaction without its pool and contractual evidence.
episode1.transferSituation: An Indian vehicle-loan pool across Mumbai and Pune.
episode1.closingPrinciple: A structured claim becomes understandable when the right, cash path, control, timing, and first bearer of loss can all be traced.
